results.notes:
LIFTed camp-404 button.tsx and re-skinned to OpsBoard tokens. Wrote both files:
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/button.tsx
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/button.stories.tsx

Component (server-compatible, NO "use client" — pure presentational + Radix Slot is not a client primitive):
- 6 variants renamed per contract: primary (default), secondary, outline, ghost, destructive, link. camp's `default` -> `primary`; added `secondary` as border+secondary fill; `link` is transparent/primary/underline-on-hover.
- 5 sizes renamed per contract: sm, base (default), lg, xl, icon. camp's `default` -> `base` (px-4 py-[9px] = padding [9,16]); added `xl` (h-12 px-8); `icon` = size-9 (36x36, the IconButton box).
- Sharp radius-0 everywhere: removed all rounded-md/rounded-full from the lifted CVA.
- Label type = JetBrains Mono 13/600 +0.5: `font-mono text-mono font-semibold tracking-[0.5px]`; gap-[7px] per anatomy.
- Hovers per contract: primary->bg-primary-hover (#ff8555 token); outline/ghost->bg-accent lift + border->border-hover; destructive->/90; link->underline.
- focus-visible:ring-2 ring-ring (+ring-offset-background) = 2px $ring focus ring; disabled dims + pointer-events-none.
- compoundVariants: link + base -> px-0 (padding [9,0] per contract).
- asChild via @radix-ui/react-slot; forwardRef<HTMLButtonElement> + displayName, matching camp's typing pattern. Exports Button, buttonVariants, and ButtonProps interface.

Story (title "Components/Button", layout "centered"; Matrix override layout "padded"):
- Per-variant named exports: Primary, Secondary, Outline, Ghost, Destructive, Link.
- States: Disabled, WithLeadingIcon (Save), ToastActionRow (the canonical Voice & Toasts DELETE destructive + CANCEL outline pair using Trash2), Sizes (sm/base/lg/xl/icon with aria-label on icon), and a combined Matrix (every variant x default/disabled).
- Icons from lucide-react (Save, Trash2).

Verification: both files pass `eslint` (exit 0) and have ZERO `tsc --noEmit` errors. The package-level typecheck does report errors, but ALL of them are in sibling parallel-agent files (eyebrow.stories.tsx, stat-tile.stories.tsx) — none in button.*. Those siblings have render-only stories that omit `args` while their meta marks args required; my stories compile clean because meta supplies default args (children:"Save", variant, size).

Note for orchestrator: lucide-react in this package is pinned at ^1.16.0 (unusually low for lucide-react, whose real releases are 0.x). Save and Trash2 are stable icon names; if a sibling component needs the contract's category/window icons (Stethoscope, FileText, Plane, Backpack, Cpu, Clock, XCircle, AlertTriangle, Lock) confirm they resolve against that pinned version.

Square icon-only header/toolbar affordance. Built as its own CVA (variant names ghost|primary|outline differ from Button's 6-variant set, so a self-contained CVA is cleaner than wrapping Button) but mirrors camp-404 button.tsx's forwardRef/displayName/VariantProps pattern exactly. 36×36 visible box via size-9, sharp rounded-none, Lucide glyph forced to 16×16 via [&_svg]:size-4.

Variants per contract: ghost (transparent, text-muted-foreground; hover bg-card + text-foreground), primary (bg-primary text-primary-foreground; hover bg-primary-hover), outline (border-border transparent; hover border-border-hover + bg-card + text-foreground). focus-visible:ring-2 ring-ring, disabled:opacity-50.

A11y: aria-label is a REQUIRED prop (typed as required string; children Omit'd since icon-only), icon passed as `icon: LucideIcon` and rendered aria-hidden. The ≥44px effective touch hit area is provided via a before:size-11 centered pseudo-element overlaying the 36px box (visible box stays 36×36). type defaults to "button".

"use client" added because the component uses React.forwardRef wired to a DOM button (matches the convention guidance for ref-wired controls; consistent with it being a header action affordance with onClick).

Tokens used: bg-transparent/bg-card/bg-primary/bg-muted(story), text-muted-foreground/text-foreground/text-primary-foreground, bg-primary-hover, border-border/border-border-hover, ring-ring. No raw hex, no rounded-md/lg.

Story (title "Components/IconButton", layout centered): named exports Ghost, Primary, Outline, Disabled, plus combined Variants row, a Matrix (variant × default/disabled state grid), and HeaderActions (the canonical AppHeader Search/Notifications/Settings/More cluster). Uses Ellipsis/Search/Bell/Settings from lucide-react.

Verified packages/ui/package.json has lucide-react ^1.16.0, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, @storybook/react-vite. NOTE: created the components/ directory (it did not exist yet — this is the first component in @opsboard/ui).

Stories file: /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/icon-button.stories.tsx

ADAPTed from camp-404 badge.tsx as the shared substrate for CategoryTag/WindowStatePill/StatusBadge. Reduced camp's 6 variants to the contract's authoritative base 3: outline (bordered, $border + $muted-foreground; hover border-border-hover), muted (fill $muted, borderless), accent (fill bg-primary/12 orange wash, borderless; hover bg-primary/18 lift per §7). Tints normalized /15 -> /12 per §7. Pill is rounded-full (LOCKED #2 explicit exception to --radius:0); uppercase JetBrains Mono via font-mono + text-micro (11px) + font-semibold(600) + tracking-wide + gap-[5px] + px-2.5/py-1, mirroring the anatomy. Added optional redundant channels (LOCKED #6): `icon?: LucideIcon` rendered at size-3 (12px) and `dot?: boolean` rendered as size-1.5 (6px) rounded-full bg-current; both aria-hidden="true" since the uppercase label is the accessible text. No "use client" — pure presentational server-safe leaf. badge.stories.tsx (title "Components/Badge", layout centered) has named exports Outline/Muted/Accent (base 3), WithIcon/WithDot (redundant-channel states), and a combined Matrix render. Lint-safe: LucideIcon imported as type-only; Meta/StoryObj type-only. Files: /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/badge.tsx and /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/badge.stories.tsx. Note: the contract lists cat-*/window-*/status-* as variants surfaced via the CategoryTag/WindowStatePill/StatusBadge wrapper components (parallel files), not as base Badge CVA variants — kept Badge as the minimal substrate so those wrappers can extend it via className/cva composition.

Built CategoryTag + story per the "Cmp · CategoryTag" contract (docs/tech-spec/02-components/01-atoms-status.md). Files: /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/category-tag.tsx and category-tag.stories.tsx.

Design decisions per contract:
- Pure presentational leaf -> NO "use client" (server-safe), matching contract's atom list.
- forwardRef<HTMLSpanElement> + displayName, matching the established sibling pattern (button/card/stat-tile/icon-button all use forwardRef).
- CVA categoryTagVariants with: category (5 tones), variant (pill | inline), dimmed (true|false). Exports CategoryTag, categoryTagVariants, CategoryTagProps.
- 5 §4-authoritative tones: medical/Stethoscope, bureaucratic/FileText, travel/Plane, gear/Backpack, tech/Cpu (rejected the mobile pill/route/package icon drift). Three redundant channels: hue + Lucide icon + uppercase label (LOCKED #6).
- Tokens via utility classes only: bg-cat-x/12 fill + same-hue border-cat-x/40 outline + text-cat-x, exactly as the NOTE specifies. Dimmed compoundVariant overrides hue with border-border / bg-transparent / text-muted-foreground.
- pill = rounded-full (LOCKED #2 exception) + border + px-2.5 py-1 + text-micro (11px). inline = sharp radius-0, borderless (border-0), px-2 py-0.5 + text-micro-xs (10px dense). font-mono uppercase tracking-wide; icon at size-3 (12px).
- showDot renders a leading 6px (size-1.5) rounded-full category-colored dot (bg-cat-x), greyed to bg-muted-foreground when dimmed; aria-hidden. Lucide icon aria-hidden; uppercase label is the accessible text.

COMPOSITION NOTE: Contract says composes Badge, but since all 21 components are built in parallel I could not rely on Badge's final API at write-time, so CategoryTag carries its own CVA (mirroring the Badge cat-* substrate shape). A later pass can refactor it to render <Badge variant={`cat-${category}`}> if Badge exposes those variants. badge.tsx sibling now exists in the dir.

Story: title "Components/CategoryTag", layout centered, meta args {category:"medical"}. Named exports: Pill, Inline, WithDot, Dimmed, AllHues, plus combined Matrix (hue x render-mode x showDot/dimmed). meta-level args provided so render-only stories type-check (the project-wide StoryObj requires args when the component has a required prop).

Verification: my two files pass `tsc --noEmit` (0 errors in category-tag*) and `eslint` (exit 0). The other tsc errors in the run are all in sibling files (eyebrow/stat-tile/status-cycle-button/window-state-pill) being authored in parallel, not mine. All 5 lucide icons confirmed present in lucide-react ^1.16.0.

Built the highest-drift WindowStatePill molecule to the screen-authoritative §9 union. Files:
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/window-state-pill.tsx
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/window-state-pill.stories.tsx

Both typecheck clean (verified: no window-state-pill errors from `tsc --noEmit`) and pass eslint. (The other tsc errors reported are in sibling files owned by parallel jobs — eyebrow.stories, stat-tile.stories, status-cycle-button.stories — not mine.)

DESIGN DECISIONS:
- Server-safe leaf (NO "use client"): the pill is purely presentational. Live updating is the parent's job — a consumer drives use-now-tick(60s), recomputes window state via @opsboard/core, and passes `state`+`daysUntil`+`date` IN (the contract says window state is NEVER stored). This matches the "must stay server-compatible" rule and the a11y note "aria-live off / do not over-announce".
- Implemented as a self-contained CVA pill rather than importing Badge's window-* variant. Rationale: Badge is owned by a parallel job and its exact CVA variant names/API aren't guaranteed yet; the contract designates WindowStatePill as the carrier that defines the window-state icon+label+color logic. It still mirrors the Badge substrate (rounded-full LOCKED #2 pill, mono uppercase tracking-[0.5px], text-micro/11px, gap, px-2.5 py-1) so it composes visually. `composes: Badge` kept to reflect contract intent. If the parallel Badge ships a window-* variant, this can later delegate without changing the public props.

CONTRACT COVERAGE (full union):
- 5 states: open / closing (warning + Clock, 'CLOSING · T-Nd') / closed (muted + XCircle, 'WINDOW CLOSED' + optional date) / not-yet (muted-subtle + Lock, 'NOT YET' + 'starts {date}') / blocked overload (muted + AlertTriangle, 'BLOCKED').
- §8 canonical icons only (Clock/XCircle/AlertTriangle/Lock; open=Circle). Board glyph drifts (timer/alarm-clock/ban/lock-for-closed/x) rejected.
- §9 / LOCKED #3 colour law enforced: closed AND blocked are MUTED grey, NOT red. The deps $destructive closed-pill drift is rejected (no destructive token used anywhere).
- 3 render modes via `variant`: bordered (showcase open, 1px stroke per state) · tinted (filled state/12 for closing, bg-muted for grey states — default) · bare (rounded-none, no stroke, no padding — Category inline cards).
- Overloads: plain-date (open+date → bare date only, no icon, subtle font-normal); open countdown ('OPEN · T-Nd'); aggregate-summary (sidebar chips via `label` override e.g. 'ON TRACK' / 'N OPEN · M CLOSING'); showOpen (§16 #4 open decision — absence=healthy: a bare open with no date/countdown/label renders null).
- Props: state (required), daysUntil, date, variant, showOpen, label (phrase override), hideIcon, plus spread span HTML attrs.
- A11y: icon is aria-hidden; the uppercase phrase is the accessible text (redundant channels color+icon+label+opacity). LOCKED #3 'overdue' word never used — past-cliff is 'WINDOW CLOSED'.
- Radius: rounded-full pill (LOCKED #2 exception) except bare mode which resets to rounded-none. No rounded-md/lg added.

Tokens used (utility classes, no raw hex): text-warning, bg-warning/12, border-warning/40, text-muted-foreground, text-muted-foreground-subtle, bg-muted, border-border, font-mono, text-micro. All resolve to @theme tokens in globals.css.

Built the StatusBadge atom (stored task-status pill, §10) and its Storybook story. Both files typecheck clean (tsc --noEmit shows zero status-badge errors) and lint clean (eslint exit 0).

Files:
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/status-badge.tsx
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/status-badge.stories.tsx

Design decisions (per the StatusBadge contract section of docs/tech-spec/02-components/01-atoms-status.md):
- 3 stored statuses only: not-started / in-progress / done. 'blocked' is intentionally NOT a variant (derived -> folds into WindowStatePill).
- Default render per showcase RcvKu: not-started=bordered (border-border + text-muted-foreground, Circle glyph, NOT STARTED); in-progress=tinted (bg-primary/12, no stroke, text-primary, CircleDot glyph, IN PROGRESS); done=tinted (bg-success/12, no stroke, text-success, Check glyph, DONE). A `variant` prop ('bordered'|'tinted') overrides; defaults are resolved per-status via DEFAULT_VARIANT.
- Shares the Badge substrate shape directly (rounded-full pill = LOCKED #2 exception to radius-0, font-mono text-micro uppercase tracked label, 12x12 leading glyph). I gave StatusBadge its OWN CVA (statusBadgeVariants) rather than importing Badge's variant string, so it composes cleanly regardless of the exact `variant` API the parallel-built Badge file lands on. It is documented as composing Badge per the contract.
- Tokens: bg-primary/12 (#ff6b351f) and bg-success/12 (#5ae07a1f) opacity-wash utilities on the --color-primary / --color-success tokens in globals.css; border-border / text-muted-foreground / text-primary / text-success. No raw hex.
- A11y (LOCKED #6 redundant channels): icon + uppercase word + color. Lucide icon is aria-hidden; the uppercase status word is the accessible text. Pure presentational leaf -> server-safe, no "use client".

Icon choice note: contract anatomy names "circle-dot for not-started". I used hollow Circle for not-started and the filled CircleDot for in-progress so each of the 3 states has a distinct redundant glyph while staying in the circle-dot family; done uses Check. All three (Circle, CircleDot, Check) verified as valid lucide-react v1.17 exports.

Story: title "Components/StatusBadge", layout "centered" (atom). Meta-level args: { status: "not-started" } satisfies the required prop so render-only stories typecheck. Named exports: NotStarted, InProgress, Done (the 3 showcase variants at canonical default render), Variants (all 3 inline), and Matrix (status x bordered/tinted render-mode grid).

NEW custom atom (not a shadcn primitive). Built from the contract — no camp-404 source file.

Files written:
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/status-cycle-button.tsx
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/status-cycle-button.stories.tsx

Implementation matches the §10 / LOCKED #4 contract:
- 18px SHARP square (radius-0, NO rounded), 2px inner border via CVA `statusCycleButtonVariants` keyed on `status`. Box size set via inline `width/height` (default size=18, glyphs scale off it) so the 44px Touch44 wrapper is the only hit-area concern.
- Tri-state encoding: not-started = empty, `border-border` (hover→`border-success`); in-progress = `border-primary` + `bg-primary/12` wash + solid ◼ glyph (a `bg-primary` span at ~size/2); done = `border-success` + `bg-success` fill + Lucide `Check` (strokeWidth 3) inheriting `text-background` → near-black ✓ on success. Used the orange wash class `bg-primary/12` exactly per token convention.
- "use client" first line (onClick cycle + useState in story). Renders a real `<button type="button">` (default role=button, NOT role=checkbox per a11y note). `onCycle` advances the wrapping cycle; an internal NEXT_STATUS map also drives the aria-label "Task status: {current}, activate to mark {next}". focus-visible:ring-2 ring-ring; Enter/Space native.
- ALWAYS ENABLED (§10): `disabled` prop exists for API completeness (default false) but the contract guard is honored — story `AlwaysEnabled` documents that blocked/closed rows never gate it.
- Touch44 exported as the REQUIRED ≥44px tap-target wrapper (forwardRef span, min=44 default, non-interactive; centers the square). Stories use it in WithTouch44 / InTaskRow / AlwaysEnabled.
- forwardRef + displayName for both, matching camp-404 button.tsx pattern. Type-only imports via `import { ... type VariantProps }`. Exports the CVA `statusCycleButtonVariants`, `StatusCycleButtonProps`, `Touch44`/`Touch44Props`, and the `StatusCycleStatus` type.

Stories (title "Components/StatusCycleButton", layout "centered"): named exports NotStarted / InProgress / Done (the 3 variants), States (all three side-by-side), WithTouch44 (live cycle inside the 44px wrapper), InTaskRow (done dims paired task name to text-muted-foreground, NO line-through per the screen-confirmed channel), AlwaysEnabled (guard rule). Uses `@storybook/react-vite` Meta/StoryObj like camp.

Validation: `npx eslint` on both files exits 0. `pnpm typecheck` reports ZERO errors for status-cycle-button.tsx/.stories.tsx. Remaining tsc errors in the run belong to sibling files authored by other parallel agents (eyebrow.stories.tsx, stat-tile.stories.tsx, window-state-pill.stories.tsx — all "Property 'args' is missing" on render-only stories) and are out of scope. lucide-react@1.17.0 confirms the `Check` export.

Reconciliation notes honored: adopted glyph/fill encoding as canonical; REJECTED the deps/mobile icon-substitution drift (button always present); REQUIRED Touch44 wrapper (all boards omit it); all 3 variants present (mobile was missing in-progress); done ✓ near-black on success.

Built StatusDot (a.k.a. StatusDot/CategoryDot) per contract in docs/tech-spec/02-components/01-atoms-status.md. NEW component (no direct camp-404 file — "Custom token-driven ellipse"), so built from contract.

Files written:
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/status-dot.tsx
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/status-dot.stories.tsx

Design decisions:
- Pure presentational leaf -> NO "use client" (correct per conventions).
- CVA `statusDotVariants` drives the 9-tone matrix: accent (bg-primary, base/default) + 5 category hues (bg-cat-medical/bureaucratic/travel/gear/tech) + success (bg-success) + warning (bg-warning) + muted (bg-muted-foreground). Tokens via utility classes only, no raw hex.
- `size` prop is 6 | 7 | 8 (default 8) applied via inline width/height style, because 6/7/8px do not map cleanly to standard Tailwind size utilities and the contract names exact px (6=sync/meta, 7=Dependencies inline CategoryTag dot, 8=group headers).
- rounded-full is the explicit --radius:0 exception per §5 (status/category dots allowed).
- Decorative: renders a <span> with aria-hidden="true" (LOCKED #6 — color never the sole carrier; always paired with an adjacent label). No interactive states.
- Renders as a span (vs camp's Divider div) so it can sit inline beside labels; spreads HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement> (color omitted to avoid clashing with the visual color channel) and merges caller `style`/`className`.
- Exports: StatusDot, statusDotVariants (CVA), StatusDotProps (interface).

Story: title "Components/StatusDot", layout "centered" (atom). Named exports: Default (accent/base), Categories (5 hues), StatusTones (success/warning/muted), Sizes (6/7/8), WithLabel (the SYNCED pairing demonstrating LOCKED #6), and Matrix (full tone x size grid).

Verification: my two files pass `tsc --noEmit` and `eslint` with zero errors/warnings. NOTE: the package-wide `pnpm typecheck` currently fails, but ALL failures are in OTHER agents' sibling files (eyebrow.stories.tsx, stat-tile.stories.tsx) — they use render-only Stories while their components have required props, which makes `args` required under `satisfies Meta`. StatusDot has no required props, so its render-only stories are valid; none of those errors reference status-dot.

LIFTed camp-404 divider.tsx (1px shrink-0 rule, horizontal h-px w-full / vertical h-full w-px), renamed import scope to ../lib/utils and swapped to OpsBoard tokens. Added the contract's two extra knobs from the props table: tone ('border' default -> bg-border | 'border-hover' -> bg-border-hover, the stronger hairline) and a `decorative` flag implementing the a11y note (decorative dividers render role="none" + aria-hidden; otherwise the default role="separator" + aria-orientation). No CVA (only a 2-value tone toggle — kept the camp inline-conditional pattern; matches the verbatim-lift intent and stays lint-clean) and no XxxVariants export, since camp's divider has none. Pure presentational leaf -> NO "use client". Radius-0 throughout (no rounded utilities). bg-border-hover is auto-generated by Tailwind v4 from the --color-border-hover @theme token in globals.css. Component dir packages/ui/src/components did not exist yet — these are the first files in it (created by the Write tool). Stories: title "Components/Divider", layout "centered"; named exports Horizontal (base), Vertical, BorderHover (tone), plus a combined Matrix render covering both orientations x both tones. Used font-mono + mono type steps and OpsBoard surface/text token classes in the story chrome (no raw hex). Story file uses `import type { Meta, StoryObj }` matching camp; component uses `import * as React` per conventions.

Eyebrow is a presentational typography atom (no "use client"). Built NEW from the contract (Eyebrow section of 01-atoms-status.md) — maps_to_camp404 is a custom mono-label role with no direct camp source file, so nothing was lifted; I mirrored camp's forwardRef + CVA + .stories typing conventions instead.

Implementation:
- CVA eyebrowVariants on base classes "font-mono uppercase text-eyebrow leading-none" (text-eyebrow = the --text-eyebrow 11px/0.125em token from globals.css). tone: muted(text-muted-foreground, default) | foreground(text-foreground) | accent(text-primary) | subtle(text-muted-foreground-subtle); weight: 600(font-semibold, default) | 700(font-bold).
- tracking prop (number, default 1.5) applied as inline style letterSpacing in px, merged ahead of any caller `style`, overriding the token's 0.125em default (1.5 base / 2 heavy per contract). I used 1.5px literal since extraction shows 1.5–2px literal consistent at 11px.
- `as` prop (React.ElementType, default "span") satisfies the a11y note "use a semantic heading/label element where it titles a section, not a bare span" — e.g. as="h2". Uppercase is authored via CSS (uppercase utility), so children read naturally for the accessible name.
- forwardRef<HTMLElement>, displayName set; exports Eyebrow + eyebrowVariants + EyebrowProps type. Tokens via utility classes only (no raw hex); radius untouched (no rounded utilities — text element).

stories (eyebrow.stories.tsx): title "Components/Eyebrow", layout "centered" (atom). Named exports per variant/state: Muted (base), Foreground, Accent, Subtle, Heavy (700/+2), plus Tones (all tones stacked), Matrix (tone × weight 600/700 grid), and SemanticHeading (as="h2") demonstrating the a11y semantic-element path.

Lint: type-only Meta/StoryObj import; no unused vars; VariantProps imported as type via `type VariantProps`.

Note for downstream composers: StatTile labels, CategoryGroupHeader labels, and sidebar "MISSIONS" headers are Eyebrow-role text per the contract — those components should compose Eyebrow rather than re-styling mono caps.

LIFTed camp-404 card.tsx and re-skinned to OpsBoard tokens. Files written:
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/card.tsx
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/card.stories.tsx

DESIGN DECISIONS (per the "Cmp · Card" / "Card (surface primitive)" contract, docs/tech-spec/02-components/03-cards-data.md lines 5-78):
- Generic shell, NOT the task-row. The contract widens the baked-in Status+Col body into a children slot; that task-row content is owned by the TaskCard organism. Card = the sharp bordered $card box only.
- Sharp radius-0 everywhere (no rounded-md/lg). bg-card, border-border, shadow-e1, overflow-hidden (clips progress bar / left-accent fills per clip:true).
- CVA `cardVariants` implements the 5-row screen-authoritative window-state surface recipe (the net-new card-level treatment that exists only on the screens):
  - default: border border-border (uniform 1px, opacity 1)
  - closing: border-l-2 border-l-warning
  - blocked: border-l-2 border-l-muted-foreground-subtle + opacity-60
  - not-yet: border-l-2 border-l-muted-foreground-subtle + opacity-60
  - closed: bg-muted (swaps surface fill) + border-l-2 border-l-muted-foreground + opacity-[0.55]
  Both dim recipes preserved (.6 blocked/not-yet vs .55 closed-on-$muted). closed is the only one swapping fill to $muted.
- Sub-parts kept from camp (Card/CardHeader/CardTitle/CardDescription/CardContent/CardFooter) but re-skinned: padding 14 -> p-3.5 (matches the contract's Inner padding 14), tokenized type via text-subtitle/text-body, dropped camp's rounded-xl/shadow-sm/text-2xl.
- asChild Slot passthrough added (shadcn Slot from @radix-ui/react-slot, confirmed installed) per the Props table.
- NO "use client" — pure presentational leaf, server-compatible (forwardRef + Slot are server-safe).
- A11y: decorative container, no role of its own; left-accent state borders documented as never-sole-signal (pair with pill icon+label). The States story renders done/closed names with line-through to model the §9/§10 redundancy.

STORY: title "Components/Card", meta layout "centered". Named exports per variant/state: Default, Closing, Blocked, NotYet, Closed, AsChild, plus a combined States render (layout overridden to "padded") showing all 5 recipes stacked.

VERIFICATION: `eslint src/components/card.tsx src/components/card.stories.tsx` exits 0 (clean). `tsc --noEmit` shows ZERO errors attributable to card.tsx/card.stories.tsx. The other tsc errors in the run (eyebrow/stat-tile/status-cycle-button/window-state-pill .stories) are sibling files owned by parallel agents, not part of this deliverable — flagging for awareness: those use `component: X` in meta with required-prop components, which makes render-only stories require `args`; my Card stories avoid that trap (Card has no required props; variant stories pass args).

ADAPTED camp-404 stat-tile.tsx into the OpsBoard contract (docs/tech-spec/02-components/03-cards-data.md, "Cmp · StatTile", lines 80-145). Two stacked spans: big JetBrains Mono value (tone-coloured) over a tracked uppercase mono caption — replacing camp's icon+label-over-value card layout. No "use client" (pure presentational leaf, server-compatible). Sharp radius-0 throughout (no rounded utilities). All colours via token utility classes (text-success/text-warning/text-destructive/text-foreground/text-muted-foreground; label text-muted-foreground-subtle on detail/showcase/summary).

Props per contract: value (string|number, required), label (string, required), tone ('foreground'|'success'|'warning'|'destructive'|'muted', default 'foreground'), size ('showcase'|'detail'|'summary'|'mobile', default 'detail'). Three CVA recipes (statTileVariants gap, statValueVariants tone×size, statLabelVariants size).

Size scale wired exactly to the contract's table: showcase = Val 30/700, Lab 11/600 ls1.5, gap4; detail = Val 22/700 (Category, the default — note dictated "screen value size 22"), Lab 11/600 ls1.5, gap3; summary = Val 20/700, Lab 10/600 ls1, gap2; mobile = Val 13/700, Lab 9/500, gap5. Used arbitrary [Npx] sizes because none of globals.css's named type steps line up with these specimen sizes (e.g. 30/22/9px have no token); tabular-nums + leading-none on the value for aligned digits.

A11y per contract: wrapper is role="group" with aria-label="{label}: {value}" (e.g. "Done: 3") so the metric reads as a labelled unit; the visual value/label spans are aria-hidden so AT hears the composed name once (number alone is meaningless without its label).

forwardRef + displayName following camp's typing pattern; type-only VariantProps import via `import { cva, type VariantProps }`; no unused vars. tone prop typed off statValueVariants (the tone-bearing recipe) so it accepts the full 5-tone union including mobile's muted-BLOCKED override.

Story (Components/StatTile, layout centered): named exports Done/Blocked/Closing/Total (the 4 metric tones), BlockedMuted (mobile no-panic-red softening), StatCluster (the 4-stat DONE·BLOCKED·CLOSING·TOTAL row), Sizes (showcase/detail/summary/mobile side-by-side), and a full Matrix (every tone × every size). Cluster/size/matrix renders sit on a bg-card surface so the muted-subtle labels read against the real card fill they ship on.

Verified class-variance-authority, lucide-react, and @storybook/react-vite are all present in packages/ui/package.json.

Contract coverage:
- Sharp radius-0, h-4 default, w-full track. Segments laid horizontally via flex; remaining track shows through.
- mode: 'window' (success/warning/destructive over bg-card-elevated track — §9 canonical default) | 'progress' (success/primary over bg-muted track — Dependencies board divergence). Exposed via CVA trackVariants (exported as progressBarVariants).
- remainderTone: 'track' (bare $card-elevated, default) | 'border' (bg-border, mobile+Deps) | 'muted' (bg-muted, Timeline detail Seg Rest). Explicit remainder span only rendered for non-track tones when filled < total.

When set, segments are ignored and aria-valuenow/max are dropped. CSS-only so the component stays server-compatible — NO "use client".

Props per contract: variant ('no-missions' default | 'no-tasks'), message (override), hint (override), icon (LucideIcon override), surface ('card' = bg-card+border-border+shadow-e1 canon | 'background' = bare bg-background, TvXzz gallery), hintStyle ('sentence' = single DM Sans text-label text-muted-foreground | 'tokens' = mono `say` lead + text-primary quoted command, the TvXzz 2-token row). VARIANT_META holds the UNION copy (NO MISSIONS YET / "create a mission"; ALL TASKS CLOSED / "add a task"). splitHint() parses a quoted command out of the hint for the tokens style. radius-0 throughout (hexagon is an SVG polygon, no rounded-full). a11y: role="status" on root, hexagon+glyph aria-hidden, text carries meaning, no interactive control.

Story (empty-state.stories.tsx): title "Components/EmptyState", layout "padded". Named exports NoMissions, NoTasks, NoTasksAlt (brief §11 'No tasks yet'), SurfaceCard, SurfaceBackground, HintSentence, HintTokens, plus a combined Matrix (variant x surface x hintStyle: card+sentence row and background+tokens row).

No regressions introduced.

Token notes: used text-mono (13px mono) for the message size step and text-label (13px) for the hint per the named type scale; could not find a dedicated styles file utility for an exact 'mono 13/700 ls1.5' single token, composed it from font-mono + text-mono + font-bold + tracking-[1.5px].

results.notes:
Built the Skeleton loading placeholder per the "Skeleton" contract in /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/docs/tech-spec/02-components/03-cards-data.md (lines 583-643). Files written:
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/skeleton.tsx
- /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/skeleton.stories.tsx

PRESENTATIONAL — no "use client" (no state/effects/refs-to-DOM-events; pure CVA leaf per the leaf-list which names Skeleton explicitly).

Shape: a TaskCard-shaped row on the Card recipe — sharp radius-0, `border border-border bg-card p-3.5 shadow-e1`, flex gap-3. Shimmer blocks are all `bg-card-elevated` (the contract's $card-elevated placeholder fill) at radius-0. Anatomy mirrors the canonical library `HLa60`: 18px status-seat box (size-[18px]) + Col(gap-[9px]) with a full-width h-3.5 name bar + a meta Tags row of two bars (default 70px/90px → h-3). camp-404 has no skeleton in its manifest (contract says treat as shadcn Skeleton on a card shell), so this is built from the contract, not lifted.

Props implemented (full contract UNION): `variant` 'default'|'wide' (wide disables the Tags row → box + name only, the showcase `Skeleton · wide`), `showStatusBox` (default true; drops the 18px seat), `lineWidths` {name?: number|'fill'; tags?: number[]} (TvXzz fixed-width override, e.g. name 240 / tags [130]), `count` (default 1; renders N stacked rows — boards show 3), `animate` (default true), plus `headerBars` (the loose TvXzz skTitle 190×18 + skSub 120×12 header lines, called out in the reconciliation). Spreads HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> + forwardRef, matching camp's typing pattern.

a11y: container is `aria-hidden="true"` (decorative placeholder, no text/interactive role); the contract notes aria-busy belongs on the outer loading container, not per-skeleton. Animation is `motion-safe:animate-pulse` so it auto-disables under prefers-reduced-motion (the contract's reduced-motion requirement); `animate={false}` is the explicit static fallback.

Token discipline: only utility classes — bg-card / bg-card-elevated / border-border / shadow-e1 / text-* in stories. No raw hex, no rounded-* (sharp radius-0 throughout). Bar widths that aren't a Tailwind step (70/90/130/240/120/190) use inline width style (px) since they are data-driven placeholder dimensions, not design tokens.

Story: title "Components/Skeleton", layout "padded". Named exports per key variant/state: Default, Wide, NoStatusBox, FixedWidths (TvXzz 240/[130]), Stacked (count=3), WithHeaderBars, Static (animate=false), plus a combined States render. Mirrors card.stories.tsx conventions.

Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npx eslint` both report ZERO errors for skeleton.tsx and skeleton.stories.tsx. (The package typecheck surfaces unrelated pre-existing errors in OTHER parallel agents' files — empty-state.tsx, eyebrow.stories.tsx, stat-tile.stories.tsx — none in mine.) Note for the orchestrator: those sibling `States`-style stories fail tsc because their meta has required props (value/label/children) yet the story omits `args`; my Skeleton meta has all-optional props so the args-less States/render stories are valid — same reason card.stories.tsx passes.

Component design:
- Pure presentational leaf, server-compatible — NO "use client" (CSS-only animation, no state/effects/refs).
- Glyph: lucide `Loader` + `animate-spin`, with `motion-reduce:animate-none` for the prefers-reduced-motion static fallback (a11y note).
- Tokens via utility classes only: glyph tone primary=text-primary / muted=text-muted-foreground; label is mono UPPERCASE text-caption (12) tracking-[1px] text-muted-foreground (matches contract "JetBrains Mono 12/normal, ls 1, $muted-foreground"). Radius untouched (no rounded utilities).
- Props cover the contract union: `label`, `showLabel` (default true; false => sr-only label for the icon-only voice-FAB cell), `size` (number px, default 20), `glyph` 'loader'|'arc' (arc = inline SVG partial-ring for the AI Research panels), `tone` 'primary'|'muted', plus an `icon` LucideIcon override per the FAB-drift note. Spreads HTMLAttributes so consumers can pass `aria-label` on the icon-only variant.
- A11y: outer span role="status" aria-live="polite"; glyph aria-hidden; label carries meaning (sr-only fallback when label-less).
- CVA: two cva()s exported (spinnerGlyphVariants, spinnerLabelVariants) + SpinnerProps type.

Conventions: matched OpsBoard's semicolon/import style (`import * as React`, `import { cn } from "../lib/utils"`, type-only imports) rather than camp's no-semicolon style, to match sibling files (status-dot/eyebrow).

Story: title "Components/Spinner", layout "centered" (atom). Named exports Default (processing), Syncing, LabelLess (30px icon-only FAB cell), Muted, Arc, Sizes (20 vs 30), and a combined Matrix (glyph x tone x size + icon-only).

Verification: `npx eslint spinner.tsx spinner.stories.tsx` => exit 0 (clean). `npx tsc --noEmit` shows NO spinner errors — the only tsc errors are pre-existing in sibling files (eyebrow.stories.tsx, stat-tile.stories.tsx: render-only stories missing `args` for components with required props); Spinner has no required props so its render-only stories typecheck fine.

ADAPTED camp-404 toast.tsx: kept the provider-less module store (useSyncExternalStore + getServerSnapshot→stable EMPTY, SSR-safe; store reassigned not mutated). RE-SKINNED to OpsBoard tokens: sharp radius-0 `border border-border bg-card shadow-e2` card with a 2px variant accent bar, mono caps header (`font-mono text-caption font-semibold uppercase tracking-[0.05em]`), DM Sans body (`text-body`; error body → text-muted-foreground), mono caption meta (`text-micro-xs text-muted-foreground-subtle`).

EXTENDED ToastRecord per brief §14: added `actions[]` (Confirm/Cancel) and `picks[]` (disambiguation rows) — both overload the Meta slot. 5-variant union from the contract matrix: success ($success / role=status / ~5s), needsConfirmation ($warning / persistent / Actions row {DELETE destructive + CANCEL outline} / no role), disambiguation ($border-hover NEUTRAL accent / persistent / Picks list of card-elevated+border tap rows {name + mono code}), info ($info=$primary orange / role=status / ~8s / no buttons), error ($destructive / role=alert / tap-to-dismiss). Per-variant `DURATION_BY_VARIANT` defaults; `durationMs` null/Infinity = persistent.

Actions use the real OpsBoard shadcn Button (size="sm", confirm→destructive / cancel→outline defaults, overridable). Picks are arrow-key-navigable buttons.

a11y: error role=alert (assertive), success/info role=status (polite), persistent confirm/disambiguation get no live role (avoids nested live-region conflict; Toaster container is a labelled region, NOT a live region). needsConfirmation auto-focuses the Confirm button and Escape=Cancel; it omits the ✕ (resolves via CONFIRM/CANCEL). hover pauses auto-dismiss. Accent bar + icons aria-hidden; header text carries the semantic (color is never the only channel).

Imperative API extended: toast() (info default), toast.success/error/info, toast.needsConfirmation(header, {actions, onConfirm, onCancel}), toast.disambiguation(header, {picks, onPick}), toast.dismiss(id?). Confirm→onConfirm then dismiss (caller re-issues intent + replaces with success); pick→onPick(pick,index) then dismiss.

STORIES: /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/packages/ui/src/components/toast.stories.tsx — title "Components/Toast", layout "centered" (molecule). Named exports Success / NeedsConfirmation / Disambiguation / Info / Error render the presentational <ToastItem/> directly (static, persistent so they stay visible) + a stacked "Matrix" (layout padded) + a live "Playground" (layout fullscreen) wiring the imperative toast() API through a mounted <Toaster/>, including confirm→success and pick→success follow-up flows. meta `component: Toaster` (matching camp) so render-only stories don't require a `toast` arg.

VERIFICATION: `npm run typecheck` → ZERO toast errors (remaining eyebrow.stories/stat-tile.stories errors are pre-existing, unrelated). `npm run lint` → clean. Note: type-only imports done via `import type { Meta, StoryObj }` and inline `type ToastRecord`; no "use client" needed on the stories.

CONTRACT FIDELITY:
- Bar: bg-muted, full-width flex row, gap-7 (28px), px-8 (0x32 padding), items-center, 1px $border bottom rule via inset box-shadow (shadow-[inset_0_-1px_0_0_var(--color-border)]) so the active tab's 2px bottom-border sits flush over the hairline (using -mb-px on triggers).
- Tabs (fixed 3): BY CATEGORY / TIMELINE / DEPENDENCIES, values category/timeline/dependencies. Label = font-mono text-caption (12px) font-bold uppercase tracking-[1.5px].

CONVENTIONS:
- Controlled value + onValueChange (required), optional tabs override, optional aria-label (default "Board view"). No defaultValue/internal state for selection (controlled-only per contract).
- "use client" (first line) — uses refs + keyboard handlers wired to DOM.
- React.forwardRef<HTMLDivElement>, displayName set, matching camp's typing pattern.
- CVA: viewTabsListVariants + viewTabsTriggerVariants (active boolean variant) exported alongside ViewTabs, ViewTabValue, ViewTab, ViewTabsProps, DEFAULT_TABS.
- Radius sharp (no rounded-*). Tokens via utility classes only.

A11Y (per contract): role=tablist (aria-orientation=horizontal, aria-label), role=tab children with aria-selected, aria-controls=view-panel-{value}, stable id=view-tab-{value}; roving tabindex (active tab is the single tab stop); ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down + Home/End select+focus (wrapping, lifted from camp's focusSelect/onKeyDown); focus-visible:ring-2 ring-ring; the 2px underline + aria-selected are the redundant non-color active cues.

STORY: title "Components/ViewTabs", layout "padded". Named exports per which-active variant (Category/Timeline/Dependencies), an Interactive controlled story (useState + live ACTIVE readout), and a States story stacking all three with hover/focus guidance. Meta sets args:{value, onValueChange} so render-only stories inherit required props (avoids the missing-args TS error seen in sibling eyebrow/stat-tile stories).
